{{Short description|none}} <!-- "none" is preferred when the title is sufficiently descriptive; see [[WP:SDNONE]] -->
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Mongolia women's national football team}}
{{Use British English|date=November 2022}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2022}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Mongolia
| Badge                 = Flag of Mongolia.svg
| Nickname              = Blue Wolves{{efn|{{langx|mn|link=no|Хөх Чононууд|Khökh Chononuud}}, {{IPA|mn|xɵ́x t͡ɕʰɔnɜnʊ́t|pron}}}}<br>Genghis Khan{{efn|{{langx|mn|link=no|Чингис Хаан|Chingis Khaan}}, {{IPA|mn|t͡ɕʰíŋkɪs χáːɴ|pron}}}}
| Association           = [[Mongolian Football Federation]] (MFF)<br>(Монголын Хөлбөмбөгийн Холбоо)
| Confederation         = [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] (Asia)
| Sub-confederation     = [[East Asian Football Federation|EAFF]] (East Asia)
| Coach                 = [[Marco Ragini]]
| Captain               = [[Tsend-Ayuush Khürelbaatar]]
| Most caps             = {{nowrap|[[Tsend-Ayuush Khürelbaatar]] (49)}}
| Top scorer            = [[Nyam-Osor Naranbold]] (9)<ref name="All-Time Top Scorers List">{{cite web |title=UPDATED: Blue Wolves All-Time Top Scorers List |date=14 November 2019 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2018/03/24/blue-wolves-all-time-top-scorers |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=18 October 2021 |archive-date=17 October 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211017225051/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2018/03/24/blue-wolves-all-time-top-scorers |url-status=live }}</ref>
| Home Stadium          = [[MFF Football Centre]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = MNG
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|MNG}}
| FIFA max              = 160
| FIFA max date         = August 2011
| FIFA min              = 205
| FIFA min date         = July 2015
| Elo Rank              = {{nowrap|{{World Football Elo Ratings|Mongolia}}}}
| Elo max               = 205
| Elo max date          = 22 November 2011, 2 March 2013
| Elo min               = 225
| Elo min date          = 17 March 2015
| pattern_la1           = _Mongolia 2023 HOME
| pattern_b1            = _Mongolia 2023 HOME
| pattern_ra1           = _Mongolia 2023 HOME
| pattern_sh1           = _Mongolia 2023 HOME
| pattern_so1           = _Mongolia 2023 HOME
| leftarm1              = 0066B3
| body1                 = 0066B3
| rightarm1             = 0066B3
| shorts1               = 0066B3
| socks1                = 0066B3
| pattern_la2           = _Mongolia 2023 AWAY
| pattern_b2            = _Mongolia 2023 AWAY
| pattern_ra2           = _Mongolia 2023 AWAY
| pattern_sh2           = _Mongolia 2023 AWAY
| pattern_so2           = _Mongolia 2023 AWAY
| leftarm2              = FFFFFF
| body2                 = FFFFFF
| rightarm2             = FFFFFF
| shorts2               = FFFFFF
| socks2                = FFFFFF
| First game            = {{fb|North Vietnam}} 3–1 {{fb-rt|MGL|1945}} <br> ([[Hanoi]], [[North Vietnam]]; 3 October 1960)
| Largest win           = {{nowrap|{{fb|MGL}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|NMI}}}} <br> ([[Ulaanbaatar]], [[Mongolia]]; 4 September 2018)
| Largest loss          = {{fb|UZB}} 15–0 {{fb-rt|MGL|1992}} <br> ([[Chiang Mai]], [[Thailand]]; 5 December 1998)
| Regional name         = [[AFC Solidarity Cup]]
| Regional cup apps     = 1
| Regional cup first    = [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup|2016]]
| Regional cup best     = Group stage ([[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup|2016]])
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 7
| 2ndRegional name      = [[EAFF E-1 Football Championship]]
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2003 East Asian Football Championship|2003]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = 7th ([[2019 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2019]])
| 3rdRegional cup apps  = 1
| 3rdRegional name      = [[Asian Games]]
| 3rdRegional cup first = [[1998 Asian Games|1998]]
| 3rdRegional cup best  = Group stage ([[Football at the 1998 Asian Games – Men's tournament|1998]])
}}

The '''Mongolia national football team'''{{efn|{{langx|mn|link=no|Монголын хөлбөмбөгийн үндэсний шигшээ баг|Mongoliin khölbömbögiin ündesnii shigshee bag}}, {{IPA|mn|mɔɴɢɜɬíɴ xɵɬpɵmpʉɣíɴ untɘsní ɕikɕé paq|pron}}}} represents [[Mongolia]] in international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Mongolian Football Federation]].

Founded in 1959, the association was inactive between 1960 and 1998 when the team did not feature in any international fixtures. The [[Mongolian Football Federation]] is a member of the [[Asian Football Confederation]] and the [[East Asian Football Federation]]. The team has never participated in the FIFA World Cup, and the only major international tournaments the team has taken part in are the 1998 Asian Games and [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup]], not progressing past the group stage in either competition.

==History==
Mongolia's first international fixture was a 12–0 loss to [[Japan national football team|Japan]] during a match in [[Manchukuo]] in 1942. Between 1960 and 1998, the Mongolia team played no international matches before being accepted as a FIFA member in 1998.<ref>{{cite web |title=Mongolian football takes a giant steppe |url=https://www.fifa.com/who-we-are/news/mongolian-football-takes-giant-steppe-70609 |website=FIFA.com}}{{dead link|date=October 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> Mongolia's first competitive matches were in the [[Football at the 1998 Asian Games – Men's tournament|1998 Asian Games qualifiers]] where they were heavily defeated by [[Kuwait national football team|Kuwait]] 11–0, and by [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]] 15–0.  

They entered [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)#Group 10|qualification for the 2002 FIFA World Cup]], but lost their opening five matches before drawing 2–2 with [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]], securing a single point. In the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2006 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Mongolia was drawn against the [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]] and though they remained competitive after the first leg, only losing 1–0 at home, they were crushed in the second leg in [[Malé]] 12–0 and eliminated. In the first round of the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2010 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Mongolia was beaten 9–2 on aggregate by [[North Korea national football team|North Korea]], and four years later in the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2014 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], Mongolia lost to [[Myanmar national football team|Myanmar]] 2–1. Mongolia then lost in the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2018 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] to [[Timor-Leste national football team|Timor-Leste]]; however, they were later awarded two 3–0 victories as Timor-Leste had fielded numerous ineligible players. This came after the second round matches had been played; therefore, Mongolia did not advance in the competition.<ref>{{cite web |title=Latest decisions of the FIFA Disciplinary Committee |url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/news/y=2017/m=12/news=latest-decisions-of-the-fifa-disciplinary-committee-2924351.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171212170357/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/news/y=2017/m=12/news=latest-decisions-of-the-fifa-disciplinary-committee-2924351.html |url-status=dead |archive-date=12 December 2017 |publisher=FIFA.com |date=12 December 2017 |access-date=31 December 2017}}</ref>

According to the voting outcome at the AFC Congress held in January 2011, the [[Mongolian Football Federation]] was suspended to conduct any activities at the [[East Asian Football Federation|EAFF]] until the EAFF Ordinary Congress of March 2014.<ref name="EAFF suspend Mongolia">{{cite web |url=http://www.eaff.com/eanews/release/2011/110319.html |title=Agenda and Decisions of 6th Ordinary Congress and 33rd and 34th Executive Committee Meeting |author=EAFF |publisher=EAFF |access-date=2011-03-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120206210019/http://www.eaff.com/eanews/release/2011/110319.html |archive-date=6 February 2012 |url-status=dead }}</ref> They were welcomed back to the federation at the 7th Ordinary Congress and 41st and 42nd Executive Committee Meeting of the EAFF.<ref name="Mongolia Suspension lifted">{{cite web |url=http://www.eaff.com/eanews/release/2014/0408.html |title=The 7th Ordinary Congress and 41st & 42nd Executive Committee Meeting |author=EAFF |publisher=EAFF |access-date=2014-01-08 |archive-date=13 April 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140413150017/http://www.eaff.com/eanews/release/2014/0408.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

For Mongolia, their next tournament was the [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup]], a tournament for the confederation's lowest ranked teams who have limited opportunities to arrange friendly matches, in November 2016. The tournament would replace the defunct [[AFC Challenge Cup]].<ref name="Committee Decisions">{{cite web|title=AFC Competitions Committee Decisions|url=http://www.the-afc.com/competitions-division/afc-competitions-committee-decisions|publisher=Asian Football Confederation|access-date=7 September 2016|archive-date=28 February 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090228175228/http://www.the-afc.com/competitions-division/afc-competitions-committee-decisions|url-status=live}}</ref> Being drawn in Group B alongside [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]], [[Macau national football team|Macau]], and [[Laos national football team|Laos]],<ref name="AFC Solidarity Cup Malaysia 2016 Draw">{{cite web|title=AFC Solidarity Cup Malaysia 2016 Draw Details Announced|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afc-solidarity-cup-2016/afc-solidarity-cup-malaysia-2016-draw-details-announced|publisher=Asian Football Confederation|access-date=8 September 2016|archive-date=31 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170831035536/http://www.the-afc.com/afc-solidarity-cup-2016/afc-solidarity-cup-malaysia-2016-draw-details-announced|url-status=live}}</ref> Mongolia finished third in the group with a loss to Laos in the final match-day ending their chances of qualifying through to the semi-finals.<ref>{{cite web|title=Laos Reach AFC Solidarity Cup Semis At Expense of Mongolia|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afc-solidarity-cup-2016/laos-reach-afc-solidarity-cup-semis-at-expense-of-mongolia|publisher=The AFC|access-date=10 November 2016|archive-date=10 November 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161110130653/http://www.the-afc.com/afc-solidarity-cup-2016/laos-reach-afc-solidarity-cup-semis-at-expense-of-mongolia|url-status=live}}</ref>

Mongolia then hosted their first international with the EAFF Annual Meeting advising that Mongolia would host the Round 1 of qualification for the [[2019 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|East Asian Football Championship]]<ref name="Mongolia to Host">{{cite web|last1=Grimm|first1=Justin|title=Mongolia to Host Round 1 of 2019 EAFF Championship Qualification|date=31 March 2018|url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2018/03/31/Mongolia-to-Host-Round-1-of-2019-EAFF-Championship-Qualificaiton|publisher=Mongolian Football Central|access-date=31 March 2018|archive-date=30 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201030163458/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2018/03/31/mongolia-to-host-round-1-of-2019-eaff-championship-qualificaiton|url-status=live}}</ref> After comfortable wins in its first two matches, Mongolia needed only a single point against [[Guam national football team|Guam]] on the final matchday to secure a place in the second round of the tournament for the first time ever.<ref name="One Point Needed">{{cite web |title=One Point Needed to Compete in the Second Round for the First Time |url=http://www.montsame.mn/en/read/16652 |publisher=montsame.mn |access-date=6 September 2018 |archive-date=6 September 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180906233755/http://www.montsame.mn/en/read/16652 |url-status=live }}</ref> After a scoreless first half, Guam took the lead in the 89th minute. However, in the fourth minute of stoppage time a [[Norjmoo Tsedenbal]] strike rescued a point for Mongolia which was enough for the team to earn the top spot in the group and advance.<ref name="Mongolia progress">{{cite web |title=Mongolia progress to Qualifiers Round 2 |url=http://www.the-afc.com/news/afcsection/mongolia-progress-to-qualifiers-round-2 |publisher=The Asian Football Confederation |access-date=7 September 2018 |archive-date=7 September 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907013545/http://www.the-afc.com/news/afcsection/mongolia-progress-to-qualifiers-round-2 |url-status=live }}</ref> Mongolia's 9–0 result over the Northern Mariana Islands set the current team record for largest margin of victory<ref name="Who is Bajinnyam Batbold?">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=Who is Bajinnyam Batbold? |date=7 September 2018 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2018/09/07/Who-is-Baljinnyam-Batbold |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=7 September 2018 |archive-date=8 September 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180908015708/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2018/09/07/Who-is-Baljinnyam-Batbold |url-status=live }}</ref>

Mongolia succeeded in qualifying past the first round for the first time in the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2022 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]] by beating [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]] 3–2 over two legs.<ref>{{cite web |title=2nd Leg: Brunei Darussalam 2-1 Mongolia |url=http://www.the-afc.com/asiancup/news/2nd-leg-brunei-darussalam-2-1-mongolia |publisher=The Asian Football Confederation |access-date=11 June 2019 |archive-date=11 June 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190611161828/http://www.the-afc.com/asiancup/news/2nd-leg-brunei-darussalam-2-1-mongolia |url-status=live }}</ref> In the second round, following a 14–0 defeat to [[Japan National Football Team|Japan]] on 30 March 2021, they let head coach [[Rastislav Božik]] go and hired [[Shuichi Mase]] as their new head coach. In their next game on 7 June, Mongolia managed to shock [[Kyrgyzstan national football team|Kyrgyzstan]] 1–0 for their first ever win against a Central Asian and a top-100 ranked opponent in a FIFA qualifier.<ref name="Mongolia edge Kyrgyz Republic">{{cite web |title=Asian Qualifiers - Group F: Mongolia edge Kyrgyz Republic to end campaign on a high |url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/asian_qualifiers/news/asian_qualifiers_-_group_f_mongolia_edge_kyrgyz_republic_to_end_campaign_on_a_high.html |publisher=Asian Football Confederation |access-date=24 February 2022 |archive-date=24 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220224150024/https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/asian_qualifiers/news/asian_qualifiers_-_group_f_mongolia_edge_kyrgyz_republic_to_end_campaign_on_a_high.html |url-status=live }}</ref> This win meant that the national team competed in [[2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2023 AFC Asian Cup]] qualifying in the third-round where they only would get the one win against Yemen.  In March 2023, Mongolia recorded its highest-ever FIFA ranking of 183rd.

== Team image ==
[[File:2019 National Sports Stadium (Mongolia) 01.jpg|thumb|The [[National Sports Stadium (Mongolia)|National Sports Stadium]] was Mongolia's home stadium until the [[MFF Football Centre]] was constructed.<ref name="Azkals now in Mongolia">{{cite web|last1=Lim|first1=Miakka|title=Azkals now in Mongolia, tired but in high spirits|date=13 March 2011 |url=http://www.gmanetwork.com/news/story/215155/sports/azkals-now-in-mongolia-tired-but-in-high-spirits|publisher=GMA Network|access-date=14 August 2016|archive-date=21 September 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160921023005/http://www.gmanetwork.com/news/story/215155/sports/azkals-now-in-mongolia-tired-but-in-high-spirits|url-status=live}}</ref>]]

=== Nicknames ===
The Mongolian national team is often nicknamed the Blue Wolves. The blue wolf is a symbol of Turkic and Mongolian people, and originates from the Mongolian legend of the blue wolf. The team has also been known as the "Shegshee", which translates as "national team" in Mongolian.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Football (Soccer) Team Nicknames |url=https://www.topendsports.com/sport/soccer/team-nicknames.htm |access-date=2022-04-05 |website=www.topendsports.com |archive-date=28 July 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170728235633/http://www.topendsports.com/sport/soccer/team-nicknames.htm |url-status=live }}</ref> 

=== Kits and crest ===
Currently, the Mongolian national football team uses an all-white uniform as their first colours, and a blue uniform as their second colours. In August 2021 it was announced that Mongolian sportswear company [[TG Sport]] had signed a two-year deal with the [[Mongolian Football Federation]] to provide kits for all Mongolian [[national team]]s.<ref name="багуудын хувцсыг урлана">{{cite web |title=Үндэсний үйлдвэрлэгч "TG sport" Монголын хөлбөмбөгийн шигшээ багуудын хувцсыг урлана |date=16 August 2021 |url=https://news.mn/r/2463222/ |publisher=news.mn |access-date=12 February 2023 |language=Mongolian |archive-date=11 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230211182326/https://news.mn/r/2463222/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

[[Kelme (company)|Kelme]] is currently the official jerseys sponsor for the team from 2023

=== Home stadium ===
Mongolia plays their home matches at the [[MFF Football Centre]], a 5,000 capacity stadium in [[Ulaanbaatar]]. The stadium boasts an artificial playing surface.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Football Centre MFF - Soccerway |url=https://us.soccerway.com/venues/mongolia/football-centre-mff/ |access-date=2022-04-05 |website=us.soccerway.com |archive-date=8 November 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221108044824/https://us.soccerway.com/venues/mongolia/football-centre-mff/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Mongolia national football team results}}
{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
===2026===
{{football box collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 31 May
| time       = {{UTZ|19:30|8}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SGP}} 
| score      = 4–0
| team2      = {{fb|MNG}}
| goals1     =
*[[Safuwan Baharudin|Baharudin]] {{goal|22|pen.}}
*[[Song Ui-young|Song]] {{goal|38}}
*[[Mönkh-Orgil Orkhon|Orkhon]] {{goal|58|o.g.}}
*[[Ikhsan Fandi|I. Fandi]] {{goal|87}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Jalan Besar Stadium]]
| location   = [[Kallang]], Singapore
| attendance = 4,324 
| referee    = Kim Woo-sung ([[Korea Football Association|South Korea]])
| report     =https://www.betexplorer.com/football/world/friendly-international/singapore-mongolia/Wznhkp2L/
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
| date       = 5 June
| time       = {{UTZ|20:00|8}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|HKG}}
| score      = 2–0
| report     = https://www.hkfa.com/en/competitions/result/49862/detail
| team2      = {{fb|MNG}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Juninho (footballer, born December 1990)|Juninho]] {{goal|5}}
*[[Ng Yu Hei]] {{goal|81}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Hong Kong Stadium]]
| location   = [[So Kon Po]], Hong Kong
| attendance = 6,016
| referee    = Hoàng Ngọc Hà ([[Vietnam Football Federation|Vietnam]])
| result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==
[[File:Garid magnai.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Bayasgalangiin Garidmagnai]] played for the national football team of Mongolia before he became the manager of the team in 2024]]

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:;margin-left:1em;float:center"
|-
!style="color:#FFFFFF;background:#0066B3"|Position
!style="color:#FFFFFF;background:#0066B3"|Name
|-
|Team Manager
|{{flagicon|MNG}} Temuulen Terbish
|-
|Head Coach
|{{flagicon|San Marino}} [[Marco Ragini]]
|-
|Assistant coach
|{{flagicon|MNG}} Sükhnaagiin Otgonbayar<br>{{flagicon|MNG}} [[Zorigtyn Battulga]]<br>{{flagicon|MNG}} [[Tserenjavyn Enkhjargal]]
|-
|Goalkeeper coach
|{{flagicon|MNG}} Dorjmoogiin Ganbold
|-
|Team Doctor
|{{flagicon|MNG}} Samatyn Bakhtiyar
|-
|Physiotherapist
|{{flagicon|MNG}} Khürelbaataryn Tsakhia
|-
|Media Officer
|{{flagicon|MNG}} Mönkh-Erdeniin Khaltmaa
|}

===Coaching statistics===
{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed"
!Name
!Nat
!Period
!Matches
!Wins
!Draws
!Losses
!Win %
|-
| [[Pavel Sevastyanov]]
| align=center|{{Flagicon|SOV}}
| align="center" | 1958–1960
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 0%
|-
| ''none''
|
| align = "center" | 1961–1992<br>1994–1997
| colspan = 5 align=center| ''National football team does not exist''
|-
| [[Lkhamsürengiin Dorjsüren]]
| align=center| {{Flagicon|MGL|1992}}
| align="center" | 1993, 1998<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.inarchive.com/page/2011-10-28/http://www.the-afc.com/ar/asian-coaches-year/29628 |title=Asian Coaches Year : Mongolia – AFC.com |access-date=28 November 2014 |archive-date=17 March 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200317094032/http://www.inarchive.com/page/2011-10-28/http://www.the-afc.com/ar/asian-coaches-year/29628 |url-status=usurped }}</ref>
| align="center" |2
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |0
| align="center" |2
| align="center" |0%
|-
| [[Luvsandorjiin Sandagdorj]]
| align=center| {{Flagicon|MGL|1992}}
| align="center" | February 1999 – January 2000
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0%
|-
|[[Ishdorjiin Otgonbayar]]
| align=center| {{Flagicon|MGL|1992}}
| align="center" | January 2000 – January 2011
| align="center" | 31
| align="center" | 7
| align="center" | 4
| align="center" | 21
| align="center" | 17%
|-
| [[Sandagdorjiin Erdenebat]]
| align=center|{{Flagicon|MGL}}
| align="center" | January 2011 – July 2014
| align="center" | 4
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 50%
|-
| [[Vojislav Bralušić]]
| align=center| {{Flagicon|SER}}
| align="center" | July 2014&nbsp;– January 2015
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 33.3%
|-
| [[Sanjmyataviin Purevsukh]]
| align=center|{{Flagicon|MGL}}
| align="center" | January 2015&nbsp;– 2016
| align="center" | 5
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 20%
|-
| [[Zorigtyn Battulga]]<ref name="УЛС ТӨР ЧӨЛӨӨТ">{{cite web|last1=Bayarsaikhan|first1=U.|title=УЛС ТӨР ЧӨЛӨӨТ ЦАГ ДЭЛХИЙД СПОРТ БУСАД ШУУД ЭФИР 8-р сар 15 Даваа 22o / 9o 5 м/с Улаанбаатар MNB Сэтгэлийн Үндэс ШУУРХАЙ: Рио 2016 Хүндийн өргөлт /эрэгтэй 105 кг/ шууд 2350 тонн хог цэвэрлэжээ Шарилж,харшил үүсгэгч зэрлэг ургамалыг устгав.. Өнөөдөр болох тэмцээний хуваарь Нийслэлийн удирдлагууд сургууль, цэцэрлэгийн засварын явцтай танилцаж байна.. Хөлбөмбөгийн шигшээ багийн хувь заяаг З.Баттулгад даатгалаа|url=http://www.mnb.mn/i/88286|publisher=mnb.mn|access-date=16 August 2016|language=mn|archive-date=25 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211025160707/http://www.mnb.mn/i/88286|url-status=live}}</ref>
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|MGL}}
| align="center" | May 2016
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 33%
|-
| [[Toshiaki Imai]]<ref name="Imai">{{cite web|title=IMAI PLEASED WITH MONGOLIA'S FORTUITOUS WIN OVER SRI LANKA|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afc-solidarity-cup-2016/imai-pleased-with-mongolias-fortuitous-win-over-sri-lanka|publisher=The AFC|access-date=7 November 2016|archive-date=31 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170831035629/http://www.the-afc.com/afc-solidarity-cup-2016/imai-pleased-with-mongolias-fortuitous-win-over-sri-lanka|url-status=live}}</ref>
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|JPN}}
| align="center" | October 2016 – January 2017
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 33.3%
|-
| [[Michael Weiß (football manager)|Michael Weiß]]<ref name="Mongolia's Best Ever">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=Michael Weiss Departs as Mongolia's Best Ever |date=26 January 2020 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2020/01/26/Michael-Weiss-Departs-as-Mongolias-Best-Ever |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=26 January 2020 |archive-date=26 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200126222357/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2020/01/26/Michael-Weiss-Departs-as-Mongolias-Best-Ever |url-status=live }}</ref>
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|GER}}
| align="center" | March 2017 – 24 January 2020
| align="center" | 12
| align="center" | 5
| align="center" | 3
| align="center" | 11
| align="center" | 26.3%
|-
| [[Vojislav Bralušić]] (interim)<ref name="Bralušić to Lead Blue Wolves">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=Vojislav Bralušić to Lead Blue Wolves on Interim Basis |date=27 January 2020 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2020/01/27/Vojislav-Bralu%C5%A1i%C4%87-to-Lead-Blue-Wolves-on-Interim-Basis |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=29 January 2020 |archive-date=29 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200129004331/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2020/01/27/Vojislav-Bralu%25C5%25A1i%25C4%2587-to-Lead-Blue-Wolves-on-Interim-Basis |url-status=live }}</ref>
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|SER}}
| align="center" | 27 January 2020 – 18 September 2020 
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0.0%
|-
| [[Rastislav Božik]]<ref name="Rastislav Božik">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=MFF Quietly Names Rastislav Božik New MNT Manager |date=22 September 2020 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2020/09/22/MFF-Quietly-Names-Rastislav-Bo%C5%BEik-New-MNT-Manager |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=22 September 2020 |archive-date=31 October 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201031131138/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/2020/09/22/mff-quietly-names-rastislav-bo%C5%BEik-new-mnt-manager |url-status=live }}</ref>
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|SVK}}
| align="center" | 18 September 2020 – 8 April 2021
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 0.0%
|-
| [[Shuichi Mase]]
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|JPN}}
| align="center" | 8 April 2021–17 December 2021<ref name="Massive Loss to Japan">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=MFF Names New MNT Head Coach Following Massive Loss to Japan |date=8 April 2021 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/mff-names-new-mnt-head-coach-following-massive-loss-to-japan |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=8 April 2021 |archive-date=10 April 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210410020232/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/mff-names-new-mnt-head-coach-following-massive-loss-to-japan |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Ichiro Takes Over Reigns">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=Ichiro Takes Over Reigns of National Team |date=22 December 2021 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/ichiro-takes-over-reigns-of-national-team |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=22 December 2021 |archive-date=22 December 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211222194608/https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/ichiro-takes-over-reigns-of-national-team |url-status=live }}</ref>
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 1
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 100.0%
|-
| [[Otsuka Ichiro]]
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|JPN}}
| align="center" | 17 December 2021–26 August 2024<ref name="Ichiro Takes Over Reigns"/><ref name="Otsuka Ichiro">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=MFF Parts Ways with MNT Manager Otsuka Ichiro |date=27 August 2024 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/mff-parts-ways-with-mnt-manager-otsuka-ichiro |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=27 August 2024}}</ref>
| align="center" | 16
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 2
| align="center" | 12
| align="center" | 12.50%
|-
| [[Bayasgalangiin Garidmagnai]]
| align=center   | {{Flagicon|MNG}}
| align="center" | 28 August 2024–present<ref name="Garidmagnai">{{cite web |last1=Grimm |first1=Justin |title=Garidmagnai, Mongolia's First Pro-Licensed Coach, Takes Over National Team |date=28 August 2024 |url=https://www.mongolianfootball.com/single-post/garidmagnai-mongolia-s-first-pro-licensed-coach-takes-over-national-team |publisher=Mongolian Football Central |access-date=28 August 2024}}</ref>
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0
| align="center" | 0%
|}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Football at the 2026 Asian Games]] matches against [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]] and [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]] on 31 May and 5 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web |title=Asian games did not qualify |url=https://oca.asia/news/7347-wpb-informed-of-two-new-sports-for-20th-asian-games.html |date=21 May 2026 |work=[[Olympic Council of Asia]]}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 5 June 2026, after the match against [[Hong Kong national football team|Hong Kong]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=GK|name=[[Gan-Erdene Enkh-Erdene]]|age={{bda|2003|8|12|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Khoromkhon FC|Khoromkhon]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Tsenguun Khandaa]]|age={{bda|2002|11|25|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[SP Falcons]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=GK|name=[[Ariunbold Batsaikhan]]|age={{bda|1990|4|3|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[Khangarid FC|Khangarid]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=DF|name=[[Mönkh-Orgil Orkhon]]|age={{bda|1999|1|30|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=1|club= [[Taichung Futuro F.C.|Taichung Futuro]]|clubnat=TPE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=[[Uuganbat Bat-Erdene]]|age={{bda|1997|2|9|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=30|pos=DF|name=[[Filip Chinzorig]]|age={{bda|2003|2|13|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Negeri Sembilan FC|Negeri Sembilan]]|clubnat=MAS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=DF|name=[[Tuguldur Batsukh]]|age={{bda|2004|1|17|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Khoromkhon FC|Khoromkhon]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Taivankhuu Khürelbaatar]]|age={{bda|1997|2|16|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Hunters FC|Hunters]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Munkhkhuslen Munkhsaikhan]]|age={{bda|2002|1|11|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=DF|name=[[Enkh-Orgil Otgonbaatar]]|age={{bda|1998|4|26|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[SP Falcons]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Otgontsagaan Saikhanbayar]]|age={{bda|2001|3|6|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[SP Falcons]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Tsend-Ayuush Khürelbaatar]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1990|2|22}}|caps=49|goals=1|club=[[Tuv Azarganuud FC|Tuv Azarganuud]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=MF|name=[[Mönkh-Erdengiin Tögöldör|Tögöldör Mönkh-Erdengiin]]|age={{bda|1991|2|23|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=7|club=[[SP Falcons]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=MF|name=[[Dölgöön Amaraa]]|age={{bda|2001|2|20|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=2|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Amgalanbat Batbaatar]]|age={{bda|2001|1|21|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=24|pos=MF|name=[[Togoo Mönkhbaatar]]|age={{bda|1999|11|20|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Khoromkhon FC|Khoromkhon]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Tsogtbayar Batbayar]]|age={{bda|2001|7|8|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[ASK Köflach]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Gan-Erdene Erdenebat]]|age={{bda|2005|8|24|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=26|pos=FW|name=[[Temüülen Zayaat]]|age={{bda|2003|12|10|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Khangarid FC|Khangarid]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=25|pos=FW|name=[[Usukh-Ireedui Baatar]]|age={{bda|2002|7|15|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=28|pos=FW|name=[[Sainbuyan Nergui]]|age={{bda|2002|9|19|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for the team within the last 12 months and are still available for selection.
<!--Sorted by position, date of birth, caps, goals and most recent call-up.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Mönkh-Erdene Enkhtaivan]]|age={{bda|1995|10|17|df=yes}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Arvinbat Mendbayar]]|age={{bda|2001|1|2|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|CAM}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Törbat Daginaa|Törböt Daginaa]]|age={{bda|1992|7|31|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=2|club=[[Khoromkhon FC|Khoromkhon]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Bat-Orgil Gerelt-Od]]|age={{bda|2002|1|23|df=yes}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Bayartsengel Purevdorj]]|age={{bda|1997|1|26|df=yes}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Khovd FC|Khovd]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Dölgöön Tuvshinjargal]]|age={{bda|2003|1|17|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Khashchuluun Naranbaatar]]|age={{bda|2004|8|5|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|CAM}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Bilgüün Ganbold]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1991|4|12}}|caps=26|goals=0|club=[[Khaan Khuns-Erchim FC|Khaan Khuns-Erchim]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 25 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Baljinnyam Batmönkh]]|age={{bda|1999|12|10|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Gantogtokh Gantuya]]|age={{bda|1995|11|30|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Tumen-Ulzii Sodbilguun]]|age={{bda|2005|7|19|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[BCH Lions]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bilgüün Tsetsegmaa]]|age={{bda|1995|2|25|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Khangarid FC|Khangarid]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|CAM}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Uuganbayar Pürevsüren]]|age={{bda|2001|10|8|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 25 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Naranbold Nyam-Osor]]|age={{bda|1992|2|22|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=9|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mijiddorj Oyunbaataryn]]|age={{bda|1996|8|22|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=2|club=[[FC Ulaanbaatar|Ulaanbaatar]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ganbayar Ganbold]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|9|3}}|caps=16|goals=2|club=[[KFC Komárno]]|clubnat=SVK|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Munkh-Erdene Batkhyag]]|age={{bda|1991|2|9|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Khovd FC|Khovd]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Sodmönkh Ankhbayar]]|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2004|10|7}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Brera Ilch FC|Brera Ilch]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TLS}}; 10 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Temülen Uuganbat]]|age={{bda|2005|5|7|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Deren FC|Deren]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|CAM}}; 11 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Namsrai Baatartsogt]]|age={{bda|1998|11|21|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[SP Falcons]]|clubnat=MNG|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 25 March 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury.
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team.
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Serving suspension.
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
:{{updated|5 June 2026}}<ref name="All-Time Top Scorers List"/><ref>{{cite web |title=Mongolia |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/123/Mongolia.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Mongolia.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:200px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1
| align=left| '''[[Tsend-Ayuush Khürelbaatar]]''' 
| 49
| 1
| 2007–present
|-
| rowspan=3|2
|align=left| [[Garidmagnai Bayasgalan]]
| 35 
| 2 
| 2003–2019
|-
|align=left| [[Donorovyn Lümbengarav]] 
| 35 
| 8
| 2000–2014
|-
| align=left| [[Norjmoogiin Tsedenbal]]
| 35
| 7
| 2009–2021
|-
| 5
| align=left| '''[[Naranbold Nyam-Osor]]'''
| 33
| 9
| 2014–present
|-
| rowspan=3| 6
| align=left| '''[[Törbat Daginaa|Törböt Daginaa]]'''
| 28
| 2
| 2015–present
|-
| align=left| [[Tserenjavyn Enkhjargal]]
| 28
| 0
| 2000–2016
|-
| align=left| '''[[Mönkh-Orgil Orkhon]]'''
| 28
| 1
| 2017–present
|-
| rowspan=4|9
| align=left| '''[[Baljinnyam Batbold]]'''
| 26
| 4
| 2018–present
|-
| align=left| [[Davaajav Battör]]
| 26
| 0
| 2016–2021
|-
| align=left| '''[[Bilgüün Ganbold|Ganboldyn Bilgüün]]'''
| 26
| 0
| 2013–present
|-
| align=left| [[Ganbaataryn Tögsbayar]]
| 26
| 6
| 2003–2015
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:200px;"|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!width=100px|Career
|-
|1
|align="left"|'''[[Nyam-Osor Naranbold|Naranbold Nyam-Osor]]'''
|9
|33
|{{#expr:9/33 round 2}}
|2014–present
|-
|2
|align="left"|[[Donorovyn Lümbengarav]]
|8
|35
|{{#expr:8/35 round 2}}
|2003–2014
|-
|rowspan=2|3
|align="left"|'''[[Mönkh-Erdengiin Tögöldör|Tögöldör Mönkh-Erdengiin]]'''
|7
|26
|{{#expr:7/26 round 2}}
|2013–present
|-
|align="left"|[[Norjmoogiin Tsedenbal]]
|7
|35
|{{#expr:7/35 round 2}}
|2009–2021
|-
|5
|align="left"|[[Ganbaataryn Tögsbayar]]
|6
|26
|{{#expr:6/26 round 2}}
|2003–2011
|-
|rowspan=4|6
|align="left"|[[Oyuunbatyn Bayarjargal]]
|4
|10
|{{#expr:4/10 round 2}}
|2013–2018
|-
|align="left"|[[Davaagiin Bayarzorig]]
|4
|19
|{{#expr:4/19 round 2}}
|2000–2007
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Gankhuyag Ser-Od-Yanjiv|Gankhuyag Serodyanjiv]]'''
|4
|22
|{{#expr:4/22 round 2}}
|2016–present
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Baljinnyam Batbold]]'''
|4
|26
|{{#expr:4/26 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|rowspan=2|10
|align="left"|[[Boldyn Buman-Uchral]]
|3
|11
|{{#expr:3/11 round 2}}
|2000–2005
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Narmandakh Artag]]'''
|3
|19
|{{#expr:3/19 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan="9"|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan="36"|
!colspan="6"|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D*|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|F|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|A|Goals Against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|F|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|A|Goals Against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] 
|rowspan="6" colspan="8"|''Team did not exist''
|rowspan="6" colspan="6"|''Team did not exist''
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy|1861}} [[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]]
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1794}} [[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil|1889}} [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Switzerland|civil}} [[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Sweden}} [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]] 
|rowspan="10" colspan="8"|''Not a member of FIFA''
|rowspan="10" colspan="6"|''Not a member of FIFA''
|-
| {{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|-
| {{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina|1861}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|-
| {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]] || colspan="8" rowspan="7" |''Did not qualify''
||6||0||1||5||2||22
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
||2||0||0||2||0||13
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
||2||0||0||2||2||9
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
||2||1||0||1||1||2
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
||2||0||0||2||1||5
|-
| {{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]] 
||10||3||0||7||6||29
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]] 
||2||0||0||2||0||2
|-
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} {{flagicon|Argentina}} {{flagicon|Paraguay}} {{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]||colspan=8 rowspan="2"|''To be determined''||colspan=8 rowspan="2"|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total!!
!{{Tooltip|0/7|Number of tournaments qualified for}}!!0!!0!!0!!0!!0!!0
!26!!4!!1!!21!!12!!82
|}

===AFC Asian Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[AFC Asian Cup]] record
! style="width:1%;" rowspan=12|
!colspan=6|[[AFC Asian Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Hong Kong|1955}} [[1956 AFC Asian Cup|1956]] to {{flagicon|United Arab Emirates}} [[1996 AFC Asian Cup|1996]] ||colspan=8|''Not an AFC member''
|colspan=6|''Not an AFC member''
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000]]||rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||3||0||0||3||1||10
|-
|{{flagicon|China}} [[2004 AFC Asian Cup|2004]]
|2||1||1||0||5||0
|-
|{{flagicon|IDN}} {{flagicon|MAS}} {{flagicon|THA}} {{flagicon|VIE}} [[2007 AFC Asian Cup|2007]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar|2011}} [[2011 AFC Asian Cup|2011]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''||colspan=8 rowspan=2|[[AFC Challenge Cup]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia|2015}} [[2015 AFC Asian Cup|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|UAE}} [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019]]
||2||0||0||2||1||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2024 AFC Asian Cup|2023]]
||13||4||0||9||8||31
|-
|{{flagicon|KSA}} [[2027 AFC Asian Cup|2027]]
||4||1||0||3||3||6
|-
!'''Total'''||'''0 Titles'''||'''0/17'''||0||0||0||0||0||0
!24||6||1||17||18||52
|}

===AFC Solidarity Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
!colspan=8|[[AFC Solidarity Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
|-
| {{flagicon|Malaysia}} [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup|2016]] ||Group stage||3||1||0||2||3||5
|-
| [[2020 AFC Solidarity Cup|2020]]
|colspan=8|''Cancelled''
|-
!'''Total'''||'''Best: Group stage'''||3||1||0||2||3||5
|}

===AFC Challenge Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[AFC Challenge Cup]] record
! rowspan="19"|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!width=130|Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|BAN}} [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup|2006]] ||rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|rowspan=2 colspan=6|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|IND}} [[2008 AFC Challenge Cup|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|SRI}} [[2010 AFC Challenge Cup|2010]] || rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||1||0||1||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|NEP}} [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup|2012]]
||2||1||0||1||2||3
|-
|{{flagicon|MDV}} [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup|2014]]
||3||0||1||2||1||5
|-
!'''Total'''||'''0 Titles'''||'''0/5'''||0||0||0||0||0||0
!7||2||1||4||6||11
|}
===Asian Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Asian Games|Asian Games]] record
|-
!Year||Result||M||W||D||L||GF||GA
|-
|{{flagicon|IND}} {{flagicon|JPN}} 1951-1994||colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|Thailand}} [[Football at the 1998 Asian Games|1998]]||Group stage||2||0||0||2||0||26
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR}}[[Football at the 2002 Asian Games|2002]]–present
|colspan=8|''See [[Mongolia national under-23 football team]]''
|-
!Total!!1/13!!2!!0!!0!!2!!0!!26
|}

===EAFF E-1 Football Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[EAFF E-1 Football Championship]] record
! style="width:1%;" rowspan="19"|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!width=130|Year
!Result
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games Played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals For}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals Against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|JPN}} [[2003 East Asian Football Championship|2003]] ||rowspan=4 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||4||1||0||3||2||16
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR|1997}} [[2005 East Asian Football Championship|2005]]
||4||1||1||2||4||13
|-
|{{flagicon|CHN}} [[2008 East Asian Football Championship|2008]]
||3||1||1||1||5||9
|-
|{{flagicon|JPN}} [[2010 East Asian Football Championship|2010]]
||3||2||0||1||6||3
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR|1997}} [[2013 EAFF East Asian Cup|2013]]|| colspan=8|''Suspended by EAFF''
|colspan=6|''Suspended by EAFF''
|-
|{{flagicon|CHN}} [[2015 EAFF East Asian Cup|2015]] || rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||3||1||0||2||6||5
|-
|{{flagicon|JPN}} [[2017 EAFF East Asian Cup|2017]]
||3||1||1||1||10||4
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR}} [[2019 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2019]]
||6||2||1||3||17||13
|-
|{{flagicon|JPN}} [[2022 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2022]]
|colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|colspan=7|''Not held''
|-
|{{flagicon|KOR}} [[2025 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2025]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||2||0||0||2||0||7
|-
|{{flagicon|CHN}} [[2028 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2028]]
|colspan=8|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|JPN}} [[2030 EAFF E-1 Football Championship|2030]]
|colspan=8|''To be determined''
|-
!'''Total'''||'''0 Titles'''||'''0/8'''||0||0||0||0||0||0!!28||9||4||15||50||70
|}

==Notes==
{{Notelist}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
* {{official website|https://www.the-mff.mn}}
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/MNG FIFA profile]
* [https://www.the-afc.com/en/east/mongolia.html AFC profile]
* [https://www.mongolianfootball.com Mongolian Football Central]
*[https://www.worldfootball.net/teams/te14238/mongolia/ Team profile] on WorldFootball.net

{{Mongolia national football team}}
{{Navboxes
 | titlestyle = background:#0066B2; color:white; border:1px solid #DA2031
 | list1=
{{Football in Mongolia}}
{{EAFF teams}}
{{AFC teams}}
{{National sports teams of Mongolia}}
}}
{{Portal bar|Association football}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Mongolia National Football Team}}
[[Category:Mongolia national football team| ]]
[[Category:Asian national association football teams]]
